back: The House of the Spirits (Isabel Allende)

back: The House of the Spirits (Isabel Allende)

front: Work: opens with 'Barrabas came to us by sea'
back: The House of the Spirits (Isabel Allende)

back: The House of the Spirits (Isabel Allende)

front: Work: title storyteller conceived when her mother sleeps with a dying gardener bitten by a snake; works with Riad Halabi after the 'Revolt of the Whores'
Work: title character falls in love with journalist Rolf Carle; encouraged to write by the transgender telenovela actress Mimi
Work: title character makes fake grenades from 'Universal Matter' to help guerilla Huberto Naranjo
Work: 'Two Words' — Belisa Crepusculario sells words; teaches a Colonel public speaking; he becomes sick after a secret phrase
Work: 'And of Clay Are We Created' — a reporter tries to rescue a girl stuck neck-deep in a mudpit after an avalanche
Work: Eliza Sommers disguises herself as a man and travels to the California Gold Rush to find Joaquin Murieta; works as cook and pianist for traveling prostitutes
Work: Eliza settles with the physician Tao Chi'en
Work: Alexander Cold and grandmother Kate travel to the Amazon; People of the Mist; Alexander transforms into a jaguar
Author: memoir about her daughter's coma from porphyria complications
Author: the slave Tete flees to New Orleans during the Haitian Revolution in this novel
